changes.to:
Frontend renders raw canonical message part JSON as plain text in chat bubbles at turn end.

## Observed
At the start of the assistant turn, raw JSON like this leaks into the chat UI:



This appears to be the raw canonical message parts array (thinking + tool_call) being displayed instead of being parsed and rendered properly by the frontend message renderer.

## Screenshots
- @uploads/IMG_8326.png — shows raw JSON at top of assistant response after user asks "What model are you ?"
- @uploads/IMG_8327.png — shows same raw JSON plus tool result sections below

## Expected
Thinking parts should render as expandable thinking blocks (or be hidden). Tool calls should render as compact tool-call pills/blocks. Raw JSON should never be visible to the user.

## Suspected area
Frontend message rendering pipeline — likely the message-to-React rendering path is falling through to a raw text display when it encounters parts it doesn't know how to render, or the message payload is being stringified and appended as text.

## Related
- oqto-dnj4 — model mismatch (same screenshots show both bugs)

changes.to:
Selected model on frontend is not respected by Pi - Pi starts with a different model than what the user selected.

## Fresh evidence (2026-05-10)
User has "MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-M2.5" selected in the frontend model picker (bottom bar shows "fhgenie-preview" / "MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-M2.5"), but when asked "What model are you ?", Pi reports it is running as gpt-5.3-codex (Azure Foundry) with Model ID Foundry-Codex/gpt-5.3-codex.

This is a live regression visible in production.

## Screenshots
- uploads/IMG_8326.png — model selector shows MiniMax-M2.5, user asks "What model are you ?"
- uploads/IMG_8327.png — Pi tool result replies "I am running as gpt-5.3-codex (Azure Foundry)"

## Suspected area
- Frontend model selection state not being included in the prompt command sent to runner
- Runner not passing model override to Pi startup
- Pi defaulting to a different model because OQTO_MODEL or equivalent env var is not set correctly

## Related
- oqto-xv6t — raw markdown leaking (same screenshots show both bugs)

changes.to:
Goal
Add a secure secret submission and use flow for RPC clients that routes secrets from client to owning runner and into runner-local kyz, without exposing secret values to agent/model transcripts, chat history, or tool logs.

Why
- Pi core cannot be changed; solution must be extension + Oqto platform.
- RPC clients need parity with TUI secure secret workflows.
- Future remote runners require locality: secret resolution/injection should happen on runner host.

Architecture
- Data path (secret bytes): Client -> Backend -> Runner -> kyz (local on runner host)
- Control path (metadata only): extension/agent commands and status events
- Secret values must never flow through chat message content, tool args shown to model, or persisted history.

Scope
1) Canonical RPC command additions (agent/files/ws channel as appropriate)
   - secret.submit (runner-routed)
   - secret.submit_status / command_response (metadata only)
   Payload (submit):
   - request_id (uuid)
   - session_id (platform session)
   - workspace_path (optional but preferred)
   - service, key
   - value (sensitive payload)
   - expires_at (short TTL)
   - origin metadata (client build/user id/device id)

2) Backend relay hardening
   - Route sensitive command only to owning runner of session/workspace.
   - Mark payload as sensitive in tracing pipeline:
     - no structured logs of value
     - no websocket debug echo of value
     - no persistence in hstry/oqto-log
   - Enforce max payload size and required fields.

3) Runner secure handling
   - Accept secret.submit only from authenticated backend channel.
   - Validate request_id TTL and replay window (request_id dedup cache).
   - Forward value to local kyz endpoint/CLI stdin path without shell interpolation.
   - Immediately zero/drop in-memory buffers post-use where practical.
   - Return success/failure + denial code (no value in error text).

4) Policy/authorization checks
   - Session/workspace ownership check (user cannot submit to foreign runner/session).
   - Optional allowlist for service/key namespaces per workspace/org policy.
   - Deny with explicit taxonomy: unauthorized, expired, replay, invalid_payload, out_of_scope, kyz_unavailable.

5) Audit events (non-sensitive)
   - Emit metadata-only audit records:
     - ts, user_id, session_id, runner_id, workspace, service/key, request_id, outcome, denial_code
   - Never include secret value or derived encodings.

6) Extension integration contract (pi-kyz)
   - RPC mode: /kyz-set must not solicit plaintext in chat.
   - Extension triggers secure client flow and awaits submit_status.
   - TUI mode can continue local masked input path.

7) Remote-runner readiness
   - Ensure backend always routes to runner-local kyz; no backend-global secret store fallback.
   - Define failure behavior when runner offline (queued? reject?). Prefer explicit reject with retry guidance.

Security requirements
- No value in logs (backend/runner/frontend), telemetry, traces, or persisted events.
- No value in command previews/tool output shown to LLM.
- No shell command interpolation containing secret value.
- Replay + expiry enforcement mandatory.
- Request correlation via request_id only.

Testing
A) Unit
- Payload redaction tests in backend logging path.
- Replay/expiry checks in runner.
- Ownership/authorization checks.

B) Integration
- End-to-end RPC submit success: secret appears in kyz on runner host.
- Verify no secret appears in:
  - browser console
  - backend logs
  - runner logs
  - hstry/oqto-log messages/events
- Denial-path tests: expired, replayed, unauthorized, malformed.

C) E2E (agent-browser)
- Trigger /kyz-set in RPC client flow.
- Complete secure form/input on client.
- Confirm agent receives only success message.
- Use secret via kyz exec/pipe path successfully.

Acceptance criteria
1. RPC client can set secret via client->runner->kyz with no transcript exposure.
2. Secret value is absent from all logs/history/events by automated checks.
3. Replay and expiry protections are enforced.
4. Ownership checks prevent cross-session/workspace misuse.
5. Works with remote runner topology (runner-local kyz only).

Out of scope
- Pi core changes.
- Full centralized secret broker replacing kyz.
- Bulk secret replication to runner hosts.

Refresh current directory on upload completion and auto-clear completed upload jobs. Fixes subdirectory view showing stale tree after upload and keeps upload row from lingering after successful write.

changes.to:
## Problem
Any sandboxed agent can read every session transcript across ALL workspaces (and other harnesses) regardless of profile. This violates the oqto-log access model ("scope derived from oqto-sandbox read permissions; no raw cross-workspace reads").

## Evidence (backend/crates/oqto-sandbox/src/config.rs)
- minimal: deny_read = ssh/gnupg/aws/systemctl only; ~/.pi unrestricted.
- development (default): ~/.pi, ~/.claude, ~/.local/share/claude, ~/.codex, ~/.local/share/codex all in allow_write (=> readable). lines ~466-475.
- strict: ~/.pi in allow_write (line ~548); deny_read covers ~/.config but not session dirs.
- spawn.rs: `_workspace: &Path` param is UNUSED (lines 26, 76) -> zero per-workspace session-dir scoping exists.

Net: an agent in workspace A can read `~/.pi/agent/sessions/--{other_workspace}--/*.jsonl` for every other project, plus Claude/Codex history. A duckdb one-liner slurps the entire corpus.

## Root cause
Harnesses must WRITE their own session files (Pi: ~/.pi/agent/sessions/--{safe_cwd}--/), so the home dir is granted writable+readable wholesale. No mechanism scopes reads to the current workspace's session subdir.

## Fix (dynamic, spawn-time; depends on cwd so not a static profile)
1. blanket deny_read on ~/.pi/agent/sessions (tmpfs mount).
2. rw-bind back ONLY the current workspace's `--{safe_cwd}--` subdir (Pi needs write).
   Mechanism already exists: strict re-binds ~/.config/oqto via extra_ro_bind AFTER the deny_read tmpfs (config.rs line ~561). Same layering.
3. Wire the unused `_workspace` param in spawn.rs to compute safe_cwd and inject the bind.
4. Generalize to ~/.claude and ~/.codex session/history dirs.

## Granularity
- Default: workspace-scoped (whole --safe_cwd-- subdir) -> maps to oqto-log Workspace scope.
- strict: session-file-only.

## Notes
- Found while building operator analytics CLI (~/scripts/bash/pi-sessions, trx oqto-qjcy). That tool is operator-only and intentionally NOT exposed to agents.
- Agent-facing session search is the correct path and already partly built (oqto-log FTS5 search_timeline); see epic oqto-2hyk.
- Add a regression test: agent in workspace A cannot read workspace B's session JSONL.

changes.to:
Implemented canonical session-ID persistence hardening for Pi JSONL imports to stop frontend history splits (messages present in JSONL but split across two oqto_log_sessions).

Root cause documented:
- duplicate oqto_log_sessions for same external_id (Pi session ID), e.g. one oqto-* platform session plus one legacy external-id session
- non-deterministic external_id lookup (`find_session_by_external ... LIMIT 1`) could pick the wrong row
- post-pass importer repair could write using mismatch session token directly, reintroducing split identity rows

Changes shipped:
1) backend/crates/oqto-history/src/oqto_log/ops.rs
   - `find_session_by_external` now deterministically picks canonical session:
     - prefer session_id==platform_id and `oqto-*`
     - then highest turn_count
     - then newest updated_at
   - added `list_sessions_by_external_in_workspace(...)`
   - added `delete_session_in_workspace(...)`

2) backend/crates/oqto/src/oqto_log/importer.rs
   - added `collapse_duplicate_external_sessions(...)` helper
   - after successful import/replace, collapse duplicate sessions for same external_id keeping canonical session only
   - fixed post-pass mismatch repair:
     - resolve mismatch token to canonical session/external IDs before replace
     - locate JSONL by resolved external ID
     - replace canonical session, then collapse duplicates

Validation:
- cargo fmt -p oqto -p oqto-history
- cargo check -p oqto-history -p oqto

Expected effect:
- no more split timelines where frontend shows only subset of messages while Pi JSONL has full history
- existing duplicates are automatically collapsed on subsequent import/repair passes

note: Canonicalized external->platform resolution and collapsed duplicate oqto-log sessions during import/repair to prevent frontend message loss.

changes.to:
## Goal

Migrate oqto from the legacy `mmry service run` / HTTP proxy integration to embedded `mmry-core` workspace-local memory files.

Target model:

```text
<workspace>/.mmry/mmry.jsonl
```

Use `mmry-core` directly from the oqto runner/user-plane. Do not depend on per-user mmry services, ports, TOML config, legacy stores, or `/v1/memories` proxying.

## mmry-core API

Use the renamed JSONL layer:

```rust
use mmry_core::{MemoryEntry, MemoryEvent, MemoryEventType, MemoryFile};
use mmry_core::agent_ctx::AgentCtx;
```

Open/init workspace memory:

```rust
let memory_file = MemoryFile::open_workspace(workspace_path);
memory_file.init(false)?;
```

Add memory:

```rust
let event = MemoryEvent::add(content, memory_type, tags, &AgentCtx::from_env());
memory_file.append(&event)?;
```

List memories:

```rust
let memories = memory_file.active_memories()?;
```

Search memories:

```rust
let hits = memory_file.search(query, limit)?;
```

Delete/remove memory:

```rust
let event = MemoryEvent::deprecate(memory_id, &AgentCtx::from_env());
memory_file.append(&event)?;
```

Deletion is append-only deprecation, not physical deletion.

## API mapping for existing frontend

Map `MemoryEntry` to the current frontend shape:

```text
id          <- memory_id
content     <- content
memory_type <- memory_type
tags        <- tags
created_at  <- created_at
updated_at  <- updated_at
metadata    <- metadata
category    <- metadata.category ?? "general"
importance  <- metadata.importance ?? 5
```

For update/edit, either:

```text
deprecate old memory + add replacement memory
```

or temporarily remove/disable edit support if simpler.

## Remove/replace oqto service integration

Replace dependencies on:

```text
mmry service run
~/.config/mmry/config.toml
per-user mmry ports
legacy stores
/v1/memories proxying
semantic/rerank memory service
```

Likely touched files:

```text
scripts/setup/18-services.sh
deploy/docker/entrypoint.sh
deploy/systemd/oqto-runner.service
deploy/systemd/mmry-embeddings.service
backend/crates/oqto/src/local/user_mmry.rs
backend/crates/oqto-host/src/linux_users.rs
backend/crates/oqto/src/api/proxy/mmry.rs
backend/crates/oqto/src/api/routes.rs
backend/crates/oqto/src/user_plane/*
```

Replace `/api/workspace/memories*` with direct user-plane/runner calls into `mmry-core`.

## Behavior changes

Intentional short-term behavior:

```text
local lexical search only
workspace-local memories only
append-only JSONL source of truth
```

No short-term compatibility path for:

```text
semantic search
reranking
named stores
global stores
session dumps
service config
```

## Migration of existing memories

Use the mmry migration script, filtering out bloated hstry/session imports by default:

```bash
scripts/migrate_legacy_mmry_to_jsonl.py ~/.local/share/mmry/stores/mmry.db \
  --store oqto \
  -o ../oqto_refactor/.mmry/mmry.jsonl
```

Better long-term: migrate legacy store buckets into their corresponding repo workspaces, e.g. `oqto` memories into oqto workspace, `octo` memories into octo workspace, etc.

## Notes

This intentionally makes `mmry` the distilled durable-memory substrate, not a second session search/index. Session search and semantic/human search should live in separate tools/layers.

```text
tags        <- tags
created_at  <- created_at
updated_at  <- updated_at
metadata    <- metadata
category    <- metadata.category ?? "general"
importance  <- metadata.importance ?? 5
```

For update/edit, either:

```text
deprecate old memory + add replacement memory
```

or temporarily remove/disable edit support if simpler.

## Remove/replace oqto service integration

Replace dependencies on:

```text
mmry service run
~/.config/mmry/config.toml
per-user mmry ports
legacy stores
/v1/memories proxying
semantic/rerank memory service
```

Likely touched files:

```text
scripts/setup/18-services.sh
deploy/docker/entrypoint.sh
deploy/systemd/oqto-runner.service
deploy/systemd/mmry-embeddings.service
backend/crates/oqto/src/local/user_mmry.rs
backend/crates/oqto-host/src/linux_users.rs
backend/crates/oqto/src/api/proxy/mmry.rs
backend/crates/oqto/src/api/routes.rs
backend/crates/oqto/src/user_plane/*
```

Replace `/api/workspace/memories*` with direct user-plane/runner calls into `mmry-core`.

## Behavior changes

Intentional short-term behavior:

```text
local lexical search only
workspace-local memories only
append-only JSONL source of truth
```

No short-term compatibility path for:

```text
semantic search
reranking
named stores
global stores
session dumps
service config
```

## Migration of existing memories

Use the mmry migration script, filtering out bloated hstry/session imports by default:

```bash
scripts/migrate_legacy_mmry_to_jsonl.py ~/.local/share/mmry/stores/mmry.db \
  --store oqto \
  -o ../oqto_refactor/.mmry/mmry.jsonl
```

Better long-term: migrate legacy store buckets into their corresponding repo workspaces, e.g. `oqto` memories into oqto workspace, `octo` memories into octo workspace, etc.

## Notes

This intentionally makes `mmry` the distilled durable-memory substrate, not a second session search/index. Session search and semantic/human search should live in separate tools/layers.

changes.from:
Goal
Create one clear, auditable, low-drift deploy/update pipeline for Oqto by converging setup and deploy onto a single release layout and contract-driven activation flow.

Problem summary
- Two active deployment models exist today (transactional releases vs direct install/copy).
- Shipped vs editable files are not encoded in one authoritative manifest.
- Multi-user drift remains likely when files are copied ad hoc into user/system locations.
- Update behavior is split across setup update and deploy paths.

Outcome (definition of done)
1) One canonical runtime/install layout with explicit immutable/mutable layers.
2) One authoritative shipped-asset manifest in-repo, consumed by both setup and deploy.
3) Setup and deploy share a single activation/update mechanism.
4) Copy-vs-symlink policy enforced from manifest classes.
5) Workdir template and ~/.pi layering documented and implemented consistently.
6) Doctor/strict gates integrated pre/post activation.
7) Migration path for existing installs without downtime/data loss.

Scope
- Oqto repo deployment and setup architecture only.
- No behavioral changes to chat/session protocol beyond setup/deploy integration points.

Non-goals
- Rewriting all shell to Rust in one shot.
- Introducing a broad root daemon.

Acceptance evidence
- New docs + manifest committed.
- setup/deploy commands produce identical release activation semantics.
- E2E checks demonstrate no regressions for personal and team modes.

changes.to: Secure secret submission: client -> runner -> SecretProvider (no agent transcript exposure)

changes.from:
Goal
Add a secure secret submission and use flow for RPC clients that routes secrets from client to owning runner and into runner-local kyz, without exposing secret values to agent/model transcripts, chat history, or tool logs.

Why
- Pi core cannot be changed; solution must be extension + Oqto platform.
- RPC clients need parity with TUI secure secret workflows.
- Future remote runners require locality: secret resolution/injection should happen on runner host.

Architecture
- Data path (secret bytes): Client -> Backend -> Runner -> kyz (local on runner host)
- Control path (metadata only): extension/agent commands and status events
- Secret values must never flow through chat message content, tool args shown to model, or persisted history.

Scope
1) Canonical RPC command additions (agent/files/ws channel as appropriate)
   - secret.submit (runner-routed)
   - secret.submit_status / command_response (metadata only)
   Payload (submit):
   - request_id (uuid)
   - session_id (platform session)
   - workspace_path (optional but preferred)
   - service, key
   - value (sensitive payload)
   - expires_at (short TTL)
   - origin metadata (client build/user id/device id)

2) Backend relay hardening
   - Route sensitive command only to owning runner of session/workspace.
   - Mark payload as sensitive in tracing pipeline:
     - no structured logs of value
     - no websocket debug echo of value
     - no persistence in hstry/oqto-log
   - Enforce max payload size and required fields.

3) Runner secure handling
   - Accept secret.submit only from authenticated backend channel.
   - Validate request_id TTL and replay window (request_id dedup cache).
   - Forward value to local kyz endpoint/CLI stdin path without shell interpolation.
   - Immediately zero/drop in-memory buffers post-use where practical.
   - Return success/failure + denial code (no value in error text).

4) Policy/authorization checks
   - Session/workspace ownership check (user cannot submit to foreign runner/session).
   - Optional allowlist for service/key namespaces per workspace/org policy.
   - Deny with explicit taxonomy: unauthorized, expired, replay, invalid_payload, out_of_scope, kyz_unavailable.

5) Audit events (non-sensitive)
   - Emit metadata-only audit records:
     - ts, user_id, session_id, runner_id, workspace, service/key, request_id, outcome, denial_code
   - Never include secret value or derived encodings.

6) Extension integration contract (pi-kyz)
   - RPC mode: /kyz-set must not solicit plaintext in chat.
   - Extension triggers secure client flow and awaits submit_status.
   - TUI mode can continue local masked input path.

7) Remote-runner readiness
   - Ensure backend always routes to runner-local kyz; no backend-global secret store fallback.
   - Define failure behavior when runner offline (queued? reject?). Prefer explicit reject with retry guidance.

Security requirements
- No value in logs (backend/runner/frontend), telemetry, traces, or persisted events.
- No value in command previews/tool output shown to LLM.
- No shell command interpolation containing secret value.
- Replay + expiry enforcement mandatory.
- Request correlation via request_id only.

Testing
A) Unit
- Payload redaction tests in backend logging path.
- Replay/expiry checks in runner.
- Ownership/authorization checks.

B) Integration
- End-to-end RPC submit success: secret appears in kyz on runner host.
- Verify no secret appears in:
  - browser console
  - backend logs
  - runner logs
  - hstry/oqto-log messages/events
- Denial-path tests: expired, replayed, unauthorized, malformed.

C) E2E (agent-browser)
- Trigger /kyz-set in RPC client flow.
- Complete secure form/input on client.
- Confirm agent receives only success message.
- Use secret via kyz exec/pipe path successfully.

Acceptance criteria
1. RPC client can set secret via client->runner->kyz with no transcript exposure.
2. Secret value is absent from all logs/history/events by automated checks.
3. Replay and expiry protections are enforced.
4. Ownership checks prevent cross-session/workspace misuse.
5. Works with remote runner topology (runner-local kyz only).

Out of scope
- Pi core changes.
- Full centralized secret broker replacing kyz.
- Bulk secret replication to runner hosts.
changes.to: Per ADR-0007. Path is client -> runner -> SecretProvider, where SecretProvider is pluggable (kyz/Infisical/Vault/env), not kyz-hardcoded. Secrets never enter the agent transcript or env in cleartext.

changes.from: NetworkMode::Proxy is config-only today; live posture is binary Open (full network) or Isolated (--unshare-net). Implement real egress capture for sandboxed agents: (1) confine agent in its own network namespace with a single host-managed veth; (2) host-side nftables DNAT all TCP to a transparent proxy (eavs in TPROXY/forward mode), force DNS to a controlled resolver, default-DROP all other traffic (UDP incl QUIC, ICMP, raw); (3) seccomp deny AF_PACKET/SOCK_RAW, no CAP_NET_ADMIN for agent. This is the enforcement half without which any credential-injection layer is bypassable via plain curl to arbitrary hosts. Note limits honestly: holds against misbehaving clients and in-sandbox processes, NOT against kernel LPE (that is the microvm tier). Design context: docs/active/design/20260609-isolation-tiers-and-egress.md
changes.to: Per ADR-0007. The egress PROXY already exists in eavs (eavs/src/egress.rs: transparent firewall, PROXY v2, SNI/Host domain ACL, DNS relay). The REMAINING work is the sandbox-side netns + DNAT redirect FRONT-END that captures agent traffic and feeds eavs over PROXY v2. Do not build a second proxy. Level-2 baseline; this unblocks truth-in-config on NetworkMode::Proxy (oqto-pgwp).

changes.from:
ROOT CAUSE: under userns-enabled profiles, bwrap (--unshare-user) writes into the host-shared workspace target/ under an unmapped in-ns uid -> files land as 'nobody' (overflow uid 65534) on the host, which the workspace owner cannot overwrite. Breaks subsequent host AND agent builds; recurs every sandboxed build.

WHY OVERLAY (not uid-map fix): COW overlay read-shares the host build cache (fast first build) while writes go to a private upper layer (zero host pollution) AND is safe under concurrency (two builders in one target/ race on fingerprints/locks even with correct uids). Overlay is already IMPLEMENTED (oqto-sandbox/src/config.rs ~1714-1774: per-workspace upper/work dirs, bwrap --overlay-src/--overlay), gated by overlay_enabled.

THE GAP: overlay_paths currently covers only package caches (~/.cargo, ~/.npm, ~/.bun, ~/.local/share/uv, ~/.cache/uv), NOT the workspace target/. target/ lives inside the rw workspace bind (source must persist). So enabling overlay alone does NOT fix this.

FIX: extend overlay coverage to build-output dirs (rust target/, node build artifacts) as a subdir overlay on top of the rw workspace, so source round-trips rw while build output is COW-isolated. Then enable in default profiles. Consequence: agent build output becomes ephemeral (per-session upper layer) - acceptable; deliverable artifacts go through explicit output paths. Add GC for ~/.oqto/overlays upper dirs. Needs a repro + real sandbox testing (cannot build/verify in the Claude Code harness). Related: oqto-b4za (closed, uid_map/Landlock ordering).
changes.to:

== CURRENT BEHAVIOR (verified from code, oqto-sandbox/src/config.rs + oqto-runner/src/pi_manager.rs + spawn.rs) ==
1. Cache + build dirs are raw rw bind-throughs to the REAL host paths: ~/.cargo etc. are in allow_write -> rw --bind; workspace is '--bind workspace workspace' (config.rs:1601) and target/ lives inside it. overlay_enabled=false in ALL profiles (555/622/688). No overlay, no redirect -> agent build and host build write the SAME physical files.
2. bwrap gets NO explicit uid map: grep for setuid/seteuid/--uid/--gid/runuser in the bwrap spawn path is EMPTY. --unshare-user is emitted only when disable_userns=true (development, strict; config.rs:1880-1883); minimal gets no userns. With --unshare-user and no --uid, bwrap uses its default single-entry map (spawning euid -> same uid in-ns); every other in-ns uid resolves to the host overflow uid = nobody (65534).
3. So ownership of writes is unmanaged: writes under the one mapped uid are owned correctly; writes under any other in-ns uid land as nobody. Runner can operate euid 0 (egress.rs:334) and there is NO drop to the workspace owner before the build, so the map covers one identity while writes occur under another -> nobody on host.
LIMIT: exact uid attribution (why nobody vs runner-user) needs a live 'cat /proc/<build-pid>/uid_map' during repro; the three facts above are code-verified. Net: today there is NEITHER isolation NOR uid management on cache/build writes.

== WHY OVERLAY (not chmod, not uid-only) ==
COW overlay read-shares the host cache (fast) while writes go to a private upper layer (zero host pollution) AND is concurrency-safe (two builders in one target/ race on fingerprints/locks even with correct uids). 'chmod-wide' fails: doesn't fix ownership (userns, not mode), doesn't fix concurrency, and opens a cache-poisoning / cross-tenant write channel (agents write each other's/your ~/.cargo). Overlay is already IMPLEMENTED (config.rs ~1714-1774: per-workspace upper/work, bwrap --overlay-src/--overlay), gated by overlay_enabled.

== THE GAP ==
overlay_paths covers only package caches (~/.cargo,~/.npm,~/.bun,~/.local/share/uv,~/.cache/uv), NOT the workspace target/ (which lives in the rw workspace bind). Enabling overlay alone does NOT fix this.

== FIX ==
Extend overlay coverage to build-output dirs (rust target/, node build artifacts) as a subdir overlay ON TOP OF the rw workspace, so source round-trips rw while build output is COW-isolated; enable in profiles. Consequence: agent build output ephemeral (per-session upper) - acceptable; deliverables go via explicit output paths. Add GC for ~/.oqto/overlays upper dirs. Needs repro + real sandbox testing (cannot build in Claude Code harness).
SINGLE-USER ALTERNATIVE: for personal/non-concurrent boxes, fixing the bwrap uid map so writes round-trip to the workspace owner gives a shared+persistent+correctly-owned cache without overlay's ephemerality. Pick per-profile: uid-map for personal, overlay for team/multi-user. Related: oqto-b4za (closed, uid_map/Landlock ordering).

CPU: small, usually unnoticeable. (a) thin per-path lookup (check upper then lower) - single-digit % at worst on metadata-heavy build workloads; (b) copy-up only when MODIFYING a file that exists in the lower layer - mostly N/A here since ~/.cargo is read-mostly and new build artifacts are created directly in upper (no copy-up).

Concurrent builds multiply DISK, not RAM. Size GC + disk budget accordingly (this is what the GC requirement above is for).

changes.from:
Regression/bug in chat composer mention flow.

Observed:
- Triggering file mention from chat UI does not insert an @file token/path into the user input.
- Result: users cannot reliably include file refs via mention action.

Repro:
1) Open chat composer.
2) Use file mention affordance (or select file from mention action).
3) Expected token/path should be inserted into composer at cursor.
4) Actual: nothing (or no valid @path token) is inserted.

Expected behavior:
- Mention action inserts canonical @<path> text in composer at cursor position.
- Works for keyboard and mouse selection paths.
- Maintains cursor/focus and supports multiple mentions in one message.

Notes:
- Related ongoing feature work: oqto-x20p (fuzzy @mention picker).
- This bug should be fixed independently so non-fuzzy mention insertion is reliable.

changes.from: Convergence work in progress: deploy supports artifact path via oqto-setup install; setup update release path now uses oqto-setup install. Remaining: make artifact path default and finish parity/hardening.
changes.to:
Unify setup and deploy activation onto ONE engine: `oqto-setup install` (the ADR-0016 / ADR-0018 transactional activation contract). Delete `scripts/deploy.sh`'s parallel bash reimplementation of activation. After this, `deploy.sh` is a thin SSH/iteration driver only.

## Concrete plan — bucket every deploy.sh function

KEEP in deploy.sh (genuinely shell's job — host iteration + transport):
- arg parsing, ssh()/scp() wrappers, host_exec(), host_exec_sudo()
- parse_hosts(), should_deploy(), is_canary_host(), collect_targets(), run_targets()
- prime_sudo_credentials(), start/stop_sudo_keepalive() (until folded into oqto-hostd, vemr.6)
- emit_event() (or move audit into oqto-setup; decide during impl)
- build_artifacts() — DEV build trigger only, behind --from-source (ADR-0018)
- deploy_via_oqto_setup_install() — becomes THE per-host path: push artifact, run `oqto-setup install`

MOVE into oqto-setup / oqto-provisioning (Rust, typed, tested):
- prepare_host(), install_current_symlinks(), activate_host(), rollback_host()
- prune_old_releases(), health_check_host(), restart_services_ordered()
- install_seccomp_bpf(), configure_trace_environment()
- preflight_host() + check_dependency_compatibility() -> oqto-provisioning::evaluate_manifest (doctor), version gates from dependencies.toml

DELETE / relocate to `oqto-log` subcommands (deploy GATES on them, does not inline them):
- run_mmry_jsonl_migration, run_oqto_log_migration_mode, quiesce_oqto_log_writers
- auto_heal_oqto_log_corruption, restore_oqto_log_from_backup, run_oqto_log_deploy_gate
- oqto_log_fingerprint, extract_corrupted_workspaces_from_error, workspace_to_db_hash
- (coordinate with oqto-t3jv / oqto-5stn — migration belongs to oqto-log, not deploy)

DELETE once socket-activation lands (ADR-0002, oqto-6k4f) — these are the restart choreography that socket activation removes:
- restart_single_user_service, start_single_user_service_fallback
- sync_single_user_unit_files, restart_all_multi_user_runners

## Done when
- `oqto-setup install` is the only activation path; no staging/activate/rollback/prune/health logic remains in any .sh.
- deploy.sh measurably shrinks (~2178 -> target <800 LOC).
- setup update path (scripts/setup/12-update.sh) calls the same `oqto-setup install`, no second activation branch.
- E2E matrix (vemr.8) exercises the single engine for personal + team.

## References
docs/adr/0018-single-install-update-deploy-engine.md, docs/adr/0016-canonical-installation-layout.md

changes.from:
Make "each user or shared workspace runs as its own container with a runner inside it" a first-class Placement (NOT a backend runtime mode — ADR-0001). Decision record: docs/adr/0019-container-per-workspace-placement.md.

Core idea: generalize the existing shared-workspace resolution seam (workspace_id -> dedicated Linux principal -> dedicated runner socket) to (workspace -> dedicated container -> in-container runner reached via bind-mounted unix socket). Backend session/routing path barely changes; placement stays invisible to the protocol.

Blocked on:
- oqto-3ct7.16 (define PlacementStore contract trait) — design container placement against it.
- oqto-3ct7.15 (delete dead container/ + RuntimeMode code) — remove, don't extend.

Downstream of:
- ADR-0018 / oqto-vemr — the per-workspace container image consumes the prebuilt bundle.

Open questions tracked in ADR-0019 (need grilling): keying (per-workspace vs per-user), remote reachability, volume/persistence layout, supervisor choice (podman-quadlet vs compose), isolation-tier subsumption, resource governance.
changes.to:
Make "each personal user or shared workspace runs as its own container with a runner inside it" a first-class Placement (NOT a backend runtime mode — ADR-0001). Decision records: docs/adr/0019-container-per-workspace-placement.md + docs/adr/0020-portability-oci-substrate-pluggable-supervisor.md.

Resolved design (grilled 2026-06-25):
- Workspace = Principal = container; Workspace = a Pod (runner container + shared infra wired as endpoints). 1 runner per Workspace. Personal default: 1 container per Account; shared: always its own container.
- Generalizes the existing shared-workspace seam (workspace_id -> dedicated Principal -> runner socket) to (Workspace -> container -> in-container runner via bind-mounted unix socket). Backend routing barely changes; placement invisible to protocol.
- Portability (ADR-0020): OCI substrate; pluggable Placement Supervisor; rootless podman PRIMARY on every OS (podman machine on Mac/Win); k8s + Docker + local-process as backends; systemd demoted to one backend.
- Drop host Linux users: isolation via container + per-Workspace userns subuid ranges; oqto-hostd shrinks to subuid-alloc + container start.
- Data: filesystem-as-truth, shared-nothing; per-Workspace volume holds oqto-log SQLite + mmry + files, single-writer-local; container = ephemeral compute, volume = durable truth.
- eavs: shared node/cluster service by default, wired as an egress endpoint; runner topology-agnostic; per-Workspace sidecar is opt-in only.
- Sandbox under in-container (reduced-cap) root: runner applies Landlock+seccomp before exec with NO_NEW_PRIVS (irrevocable even by root); no CAP_SYS_ADMIN; cross-tenant (userns) and intra-Workspace (landlock) are independent layers; capability advertisement + graceful degradation where Landlock absent.
- Operability is a first-class deliverable (labels + oqtoctl debug verbs + registry); transparency relocates host -> control plane.

Blocked on: oqto-3ct7.16 (PlacementStore contract), oqto-3ct7.15 (delete dead container/RuntimeMode code).
Downstream of: ADR-0018 / oqto-vemr.9 (complete prebuilt bundle feeds the per-Workspace image).

Open: remote reachability (dial-in), mount-source future types, resource governance.

changes.to:
Enforcement half of ADR-0021. Implemented as ordered sub-steps; CROSS-REPO (byteowlz/byt + byteowlz/.github). Decisions: docs/adr/0021 (spec) + docs/adr/0022 (byt distribution).

Sub-steps (in order):
1. Decide+bootstrap byt distribution (ADR-0022): build container image ghcr.io/byteowlz/byt bundling byt + cargo-zigbuild/zig + rust + go, from source ONCE (one-off byt-repo workflow or local docker build), push to GHCR (private; pulled from same-org Actions via GITHUB_TOKEN). byt is private with 0 releases today, so this is the unlock.
   Interim option to prove the flow before the image exists: cargo install --git (with token) on a rust runner.
2. Implement the `byt release` subcommand per ADR-0021:
   - subcommands: build [--target T] / checksums / publish [--tag] / verify <dir|url> / all-in-one; --dry-run everywhere.
   - per-repo byt.release.toml: name, bins[], targets[], extra[], sign, lang.
   - delegate ONLY compile to language backends (cargo-zigbuild for Rust, go for Go); byt owns staging (top-level {name}-v{ver}-{triple}/ dir with bin/ + LICENSE/README), tar.gz naming, checksums.txt, optional signing, gh release publish.
   - after first image, byt self-hosts its own releases.
3. Reusable workflow byteowlz/.github/.github/workflows/release.yml: job runs `container: ghcr.io/byteowlz/byt:vN`, checkout, `byt release` (GITHUB_TOKEN). Consumer repos call it pinned to a tag (~3-line release.yml).

Caveat: container: jobs are Linux-only -> covers required linux x86_64/aarch64 (zigbuild). Native darwin/windows need a prebuilt byt binary via a byteowlz/setup-byt action; defer until wanted.

Done when: a tag push on a conforming repo produces spec-shaped artifacts (oqto-setup acquire verifies them end-to-end).

changes.from: Author the ~20-line paths helper implementing the epic rule (XDG_* wins any OS; ~/.config-style on unix incl mac; APPDATA on win; dirs crate only for Windows defaults). Functions: config_dir/data_dir/state_dir/cache_dir(app). Plus a one-paragraph convention note (home: byt docs or a docs/ note). This is vendored into each Rust tool; Go tools get the equivalent. Unblocks the per-tool adoptions below.
changes.to:
The helper ALREADY EXISTS and is vendored into ~20+ repos, originating from templates/rust-workspace/crates/rust-core/src/paths.rs (the scaffold). So this is fix-existing + propagate, NOT write-new.

Bug: default_config_dir/data_dir/state_dir fall through to dirs::config_dir()/data_dir() which return ~/Library/Application Support on macOS (unwanted for CLI tools); meanwhile dirs::state_dir() is None on macOS so STATE falls through to ~/.local/state -> same app splits files on mac. Option B makes all four consistent.

Fix (surgical, per function): gate the dirs::* fallback behind #[cfg(windows)] so unix (incl macOS) always uses the ~/.X path; use dirs::data_local_dir for the Windows state dir (%LOCALAPPDATA%). XDG_* env var still wins first on every OS.

Steps:
1. Fix templates/rust-workspace/crates/rust-core/src/paths.rs (source of truth -> new scaffolds correct).
2. Re-propagate corrected paths.rs to the ~20 vendored copies (dgrmr, castR, hdscl, helmet, kyz, lnkdn, mailz, ocr, persona, rlmz, rvrsd, skdlr, tmz, twttr, whtspp, gvnr, ...). Candidate: a byt cross-repo sync (like design-system/schema sync) rather than 20 manual edits.

changes.from:
Make byteowlz tools truly cross-platform (audit 2026-06-28). Cross-repo; tracked from the oqto tracker.

CANONICAL CONFIG-DIR RULE (option B, decided): an explicit XDG_* env var wins on EVERY OS; otherwise ~/.config-style on all unix INCLUDING macOS (deliberately NOT ~/Library/Application Support for CLI tools), and native APPDATA/LOCALAPPDATA on Windows.

  config:  $XDG_CONFIG_HOME | ~/.config        (unix incl mac) | %APPDATA%      (win)
  data:    $XDG_DATA_HOME   | ~/.local/share   (unix incl mac) | %APPDATA%      (win)
  state:   $XDG_STATE_HOME  | ~/.local/state   (unix incl mac) | %LOCALAPPDATA% (win)
  cache:   $XDG_CACHE_HOME  | ~/.cache         (unix incl mac) | %LOCALAPPDATA% (win)

Delivery: a small vendored paths helper (~20 lines, uses the dirs crate only for the Windows defaults; never dirs::config_dir on mac, which would give ~/Library). Vendored per-repo (consistent with ADR-0017 vendor-not-publish), NOT a shared published crate.

Audit verdicts: truly cross-platform already = agntz, ignr, skdlr, tmpltr, scrpr. Needs work = mmry (hard Windows compile blocker), eavs, trx, sx, byt, sldr (cosmetic). macOS already works for ALL tools; Windows is the only real frontier.
